text:
I opened **15 new GitHub issues** in the `acme/web` repository.

Each issue has a title in the format `Verify: <PR title>`, where the `<PR title>` comes from a merged PR that closes a Linear issue. The issues were created from pull requests across all repositories (I did not filter by repository - any merged PR with a `closes_linear` value was processed).

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, state, labels)
SELECT 
  'acme/web' AS repo,
  'Verify: ' || title AS title,
  'open' AS state,
  '' AS labels
FROM github_pull_requests
WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL
RETURNING id, repo, title"}

message: MemoryBackend: unexpected token "RETURNING" after a complete statement (did an earlier clause end early, or is this an unsupported feature?)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, state, labels)
SELECT 
  'acme/web' AS repo,
  'Verify: ' || title AS title,
  'open' AS state,
  '' AS labels
FROM github_pull_requests
WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM github_issues WHERE title LIKE 'Verify: %'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, repo, title FROM github_issues WHERE title LIKE 'Verify: %' ORDER BY id"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT number, repo, title FROM github_issues WHERE title LIKE 'Verify: %' ORDER BY number"}
